title: Ben's Container plaques DARK MODE
description: Adds fancy plaques to the top of container UI's.
body:
![Container Plaques!](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/bbb29a3f83047ad859d13ae28e99b78599bbaa3e.png) # About Adds fancy plaques to the top of container GUI's!
Also available in **[Light Mode!](https://modrinth.com/project/bens-container-plaques)**
Also check **dependencies** on Versions tab
![Crafting table GUI with the plaque](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/d27e8c177a49f8f112bfb9f78a31af73cac26a88_0.webp) # View all:
all plaques: ![All of the container plaques](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/c1a7513a6b38350f0d2f7333b3dfebb2f5ad90ba.png)
## FAQ / Issues: - Incompatible with other resoruce packs that change container GUI due to how resoruce packs work. If you want to have the plaques with another pack, you will have to combine the textures in microsoft paint or something - If there is white text on the GUI that dosen't look like it's supposed to be there, then go to ```.minecraft\resourcepacks\Ben's Container Plaques\assets\minecraft\lang``` and add new .json file for the language you are using in Minecraft. I use [this site](https://mcasset.cloud/1.21.5/assets/minecraft/lang), but you can get it from wherever you like. Now, open it in notepad or whatever and find (ctrl + F) "container". Once you get past the commands and reach something that looks like ```"container.beacon": "Beacon"```, then remove the text in the brackets so it will say ```"container.beacon": ""```. Do this for all the containers that have textures in the pack. Save the file and reload textures on Minecraft by pressing F3+T and it should work. ### Contact @bennymccarthy on Discord if you find any issues or want to reach out
